edges.description: Loss or inactivity of CTSK/cathepsin K in osteoclasts impairs degradation of type I collagen and other organic bone matrix proteins during bone resorption.
Failure to digest demineralized type I collagen matrix produces osteosclerosis, abnormal bone architecture, and fracture susceptibility.
Impaired osteoclast collagen degradation alters skull, clavicle, facial, and mandibular remodeling, producing delayed closure of sutures and characteristic craniofacial morphology.
intermediate_mechanisms: impaired cranial and mandibular bone remodeling

edges.description: Abnormal jaw, digit, nail, and dental development follows the same impaired skeletal matrix remodeling process and contributes to dental crowding, malocclusion, and obstructive sleep apnea.
Increased bone density is the skeletal readout of impaired organic matrix turnover.
Defective skeletal remodeling and osteosclerotic bone dysplasia contribute to short stature.
Dense but structurally abnormal bone predisposes to pathologic and recurrent fractures.
Distal phalangeal bone remodeling abnormalities produce terminal phalangeal osteolysis.
Skull remodeling defects delay anterior fontanelle closure.
edges.target: Delayed closure of the anterior fontanelle

edges.description: Impaired skull remodeling leaves cranial sutures persistently open.
Altered craniofacial modeling produces frontal bossing.
Maxillary and midfacial hypoplasia reflect abnormal craniofacial remodeling.
Mandibular remodeling defects produce a small mandible.
Mandibular modeling abnormalities produce an obtuse mandibular angle.
Skeletal remodeling abnormalities involve the clavicles.
Blue sclerae accompany the craniofacial phenotype in pycnodysostosis.
Orbital craniofacial morphology contributes to ocular proptosis.
Distal appendicular skeletal involvement produces short digits.
Distal digit involvement includes dysplastic nails.
intermediate_mechanisms: distal digit skeletal and appendageal involvement

edges.description: Abnormal craniofacial and dental development delays tooth eruption.
Dental developmental abnormalities include enamel hypoplasia.
Maxillomandibular skeletal constraints contribute to dental crowding.
Jaw remodeling abnormalities produce malocclusion.
Craniofacial and upper-airway anatomy predispose to obstructive sleep apnea.
intermediate_mechanisms: upper-airway narrowing from craniofacial skeletal morphology

edges.description: Maxillary and oral skeletal remodeling abnormalities produce a narrow or grooved palate.

nodes.description: Resorption of the terminal phalanges is a cardinal distinguishing feature of pycnodysostosis.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis at otorhinolaryngology.
evidence.snippet: acroosteolysis of the distal phalanges.

nodes.description: Blue sclerae are part of the characteristic craniofacial and ocular appearance described in pycnodysostosis.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis at otorhinolaryngology.
evidence.snippet: characteristic facial appearance (delayed closure of fontanelles and cranial sutures, mandibular hypoplasia and angle disorder, blue sclera)

nodes.description: Short digits of the hands and feet are recurrent appendicular findings in pycnodysostosis.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis at otorhinolaryngology.
evidence.snippet: All patients had facial dysmorphism with frontal bossing and the hands and feet had short digits with overlying cutaneous wrinkles that tapered off with large overriding nails.

nodes.description: Cathepsin K is a lysosomal cysteine protease with highest expression in osteoclasts. Loss-of-function mutations cause total loss or inactivity of cathepsin K protein. Hot mutation spots are found in exons 6 and 7, with 70% of mutations in the mature domain.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis, a lysosomal disease caused by cathepsin K deficiency.
evidence.snippet: pycnodysostosis results from gene defects in a lysosomal protease with highest expression in osteoclasts

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and animal research findings in pycnodysostosis and gene mutations of cathepsin K from 1996 to 2011.
evidence.snippet: CTSK mutations result in total loss or inactivity of the CTSK protein, which causes abnormal degradation of bone matrix proteins such as type I collagen

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and animal research findings in pycnodysostosis and gene mutations of cathepsin K from 1996 to 2011.
evidence.snippet: The hot mutation spots are found in exons 6 and 7

nodes.description: Abnormal clavicular morphology, particularly involving the acromial ends, is reported in affected children.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: clinical, radiologic, and endocrine evaluation and linear growth after growth hormone therapy.
evidence.snippet: dysplastic acromial ends of the clavicles (6 of 8)

nodes.description: Osteoclast collagen-degradation failure disrupts cranial vault, mandibular, maxillary, clavicular, and orbital skeletal modeling, producing delayed fontanelle and suture closure, frontal bossing, midface retrusion, micrognathia, obtuse mandibular angle, clavicular dysplasia, blue sclerae, and proptosis.

evidence.snippet: characteristic facial appearance (delayed closure of fontanelles and cranial sutures, mandibular hypoplasia and angle disorder, blue sclera)

nodes.description: Persistence of a large anterior fontanelle beyond the expected age is a common cranial finding.
nodes.id: Delayed closure of the anterior fontanelle

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: clinical, radiologic, and endocrine evaluation and linear growth after growth hormone therapy.
evidence.snippet: large fontanel with delayed closure (8 of 8)

nodes.description: Persistently open or delayed-closing cranial sutures are a hallmark skull abnormality in pycnodysostosis.

evidence.reference_title: Oral and maxillofacial manifestations of Pycnodysostosis: a summary of clinical, radiological and cephalometric diagnostic criteria. A systematic review.
evidence.snippet: unclosed cranial sutures (97 %)

nodes.description: Delayed eruption affects the dentition and contributes to the characteristic oral phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: clinical, radiologic, and endocrine evaluation and linear growth after growth hormone therapy.
evidence.snippet: delayed teeth eruption (8 of 8)

nodes.description: Marked crowding is a recurrent orthodontic problem in pycnodysostosis.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: Characteristics of teeth, mouth and jaws.
evidence.snippet: dental crowding, malocclusion (anterior crossbite, posterior open bite)

nodes.description: Malocclusion, including anterior crossbite and posterior open bite, is a recurrent dentofacial manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: Characteristics of teeth, mouth and jaws.
evidence.snippet: malocclusion (anterior crossbite, posterior open bite)

nodes.description: The same collagen-rich skeletal matrix turnover defect affects distal phalanges, nails, teeth, maxillomandibular alignment, and the upper airway, producing brachydactyly, nail dysplasia, delayed tooth eruption, enamel hypoplasia, dental crowding, malocclusion, and obstructive sleep apnea.

evidence.snippet: All patients had facial dysmorphism with frontal bossing and the hands and feet had short digits with overlying cutaneous wrinkles that tapered off with large overriding nails.

nodes.description: Enamel hypoplasia is part of the recurrent dental phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: clinical, radiologic, and endocrine evaluation and linear growth after growth hormone therapy.
evidence.snippet: enamel hypoplasia (7 of 8)

nodes.description: Frontal bossing is a recurrent component of the characteristic craniofacial appearance.

evidence.reference_title: Oral and maxillofacial manifestations of Pycnodysostosis: a summary of clinical, radiological and cephalometric diagnostic criteria. A systematic review.
evidence.snippet: frontal bossing (92 %)

nodes.description: Diffuse increased bone density is a core radiographic feature of pycnodysostosis.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis, a lysosomal disease caused by cathepsin K deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Pycnodysostosis, an autosomal recessive osteochondrodysplasia characterized by osteosclerosis and short stature, maps to chromosome 1q21.

nodes.description: Cathepsin K is the major protease responsible for degradation of type I collagen in the bone matrix during bone resorption. Unlike osteopetrosis, osteoclasts in pycnodysostosis can acidify the resorption lacuna normally but cannot degrade the demineralized organic matrix. This leads to accumulation of undigested collagen matrix and osteosclerosis.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis, a lysosomal disease caused by cathepsin K deficiency.
evidence.snippet: cathepsin K is a major protease in bone resorption

nodes.description: A small mandible is a frequent feature of the pycnodysostosis facial phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Oral and maxillofacial manifestations of Pycnodysostosis: a summary of clinical, radiological and cephalometric diagnostic criteria. A systematic review.
evidence.snippet: micrognathia (92 %)

nodes.description: Maxillary and midfacial hypoplasia produce a retrusive middle third of the face.

evidence.reference_title: Oral and maxillofacial manifestations of Pycnodysostosis: a summary of clinical, radiological and cephalometric diagnostic criteria. A systematic review.
evidence.snippet: midfacial hypoplasia (100 %)

nodes.description: Dysplastic nails are a recurrent appendageal feature in affected children.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: clinical, radiologic, and endocrine evaluation and linear growth after growth hormone therapy.
evidence.snippet: dysplastic nails (8 of 8)

nodes.description: A narrow or grooved palate is a frequent oral/maxillofacial finding in pycnodysostosis and reflects abnormal maxillofacial skeletal remodeling.

evidence.reference_title: Oral and maxillofacial manifestations of Pycnodysostosis: a summary of clinical, radiological and cephalometric diagnostic criteria. A systematic review.
evidence.snippet: narrow and grooved palate (100-73 %)

nodes.description: Upper-airway narrowing can lead to clinically significant obstructive sleep apnea.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis at otorhinolaryngology.
evidence.snippet: Video recordings showed that six of the eight patients (75%) had respiratory distress and four (50%) had sleep apnea.

nodes.description: Loss of the normal gonial angle is a diagnostically important mandibular abnormality.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: clinical, radiologic, and endocrine evaluation and linear growth after growth hormone therapy.
evidence.snippet: obtuse mandibular angle (8 of 8)

nodes.description: Matrix retention and defective osteoclast-mediated remodeling cause dense but brittle bone, producing generalized osteosclerosis, pathologic fractures, short stature, and distal phalangeal osteolysis.

evidence.snippet: including short stature, an increase in bone density with pathologic fractures

nodes.description: Prominent eyes or proptosis occur as part of the characteristic craniofacial phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Pycnodysostosis: clinical, radiologic, and endocrine evaluation and linear growth after growth hormone therapy.
evidence.snippet: ocular proptosis (8 of 8)

nodes.description: Fragile sclerotic bone predisposes affected individuals to pathologic or recurrent fractures.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and animal research findings in pycnodysostosis and gene mutations of cathepsin K from 1996 to 2011.
evidence.snippet: an increase in bone density with pathologic fractures

nodes.description: Short stature is a characteristic growth phenotype in affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and animal research findings in pycnodysostosis and gene mutations of cathepsin K from 1996 to 2011.
evidence.snippet: including short stature, an increase in bone density with pathologic fractures
